methods.description: Quantitative evaluation of cranial anatomy of glyptodont specimens using direct measurements with physical tools and measurements/volume calculations from 3D reconstructions obtained from digital scans. This method provides the numerical data used for quantitative comparisons.

steps.description: All measurements were made directly on the specimens using a Vernier Caliper with a precision of 0.05 mm and a Protractor.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform direct linear and angular measurements on physical specimens using caliper and protractor.

steps.description: When specimens were incomplete, we produced estimates from complete specimens in the literature based on at least one comparable measurement.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate measurements for incomplete specimens based on literature data from complete specimens using comparable measurements.

steps.description: For the endocranial part, two linear measurements of the 3D braincase endocast... were obtained using MIMICS v. 23.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain linear measurements from 3D braincase endocasts using specialized software (MIMICS).

steps.description: ...two linear and one new angular measurement of the inner ear (see Billet et al., 2012Billet et al., , 2015aBillet et al., , 2015b))... were obtained using MIMICS v. 23.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain linear and angular measurements from 3D inner ear models using specialized software (MIMICS).

steps.description: ...the total raw volume of the 3D braincase endocast... were obtained using MIMICS v. 23.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate total raw volume of 3D braincase endocasts using specialized software (MIMICS).
outputs: Total raw volume of braincase endocast

steps.description: ...and the relative volumes (i.e., divided by the total raw volume) of the olfactory bulbs, cerebrum, and cerebellum were obtained using MIMICS v. 23.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative volumes of brain regions (olfactory bulbs, cerebrum, cerebellum) from 3D endocasts using specialized software (MIMICS).

steps.description: (Implied from Table 2 mention) Record all obtained measurements and estimations.
steps.searchable_summary: Record all direct, estimated, and 3D-derived measurements and volumes in a structured format (e.g., table).

methods.method_name: Specimen Sampling and Digital Data Acquisition
methods.description: Process of selecting glyptodont specimens from museum collections and acquiring digital representations (μCT scans, surface scans) for subsequent 3D reconstruction and analysis. This provides the raw data for the study.

steps.description: Specimens used in the present study are housed at the PIMUZ (Palaeontological collections of the Department of Paleontology, University of Zurich) and at the ZMK (Zoologisk Museum, København, Denmark, included in the Lausen Collection).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and access physical specimens from specified museum collections (PIMUZ, ZMK).

steps.description: We studied also glyptodonts housed at the MCGL (Musée Cantonal de Géologie Lausanne) and the MHNG (Museum d'Histoire Naturelle de Genève), but these were the same species as those stored at PIMUZ and ZMK, showed no significant intraspecific variation, and sometimes were much more fragmentary, thus, we do not report on them. Two almost complete skulls (= cranium + mandible), four incomplete crania, and five incomplete mandibles were examined from the PIMUZ collection (Table 1). We also studied three almost complete skulls of Glyptodon munizi, Neosclerocalyptus ornatus, and Panochthus intermedius at the ZMK (Table 1)... The total number of specimens (n = 13) from Roth collection corresponds to seven species of glyptodonts...
steps.searchable_summary: Select specific specimens from available collections based on criteria such as completeness, species representation, and collection source.
outputs: List of selected specimens for study

steps.description: Digital data for a major part of our specimens from the PIMUZ were acquired using X-ray micro-computed tomography (μCT). Specimens were scanned at the Irchel Campus of the University of Zurich using Nikon XT H 225 ST.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire digital data of selected specimens using X-ray micro-computed tomography (μCT) scanning.

steps.description: In addition, the three specimens from the ZMK were acquired by surface scanning using an Artec Spider.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire digital data of selected specimens using surface scanning.
outputs: Surface scan data (point clouds or meshes)

steps.description: Three-dimensional reconstructions of the selected structures (see below) were performed using stacks of digital μCT images with MIMICS v. 23.0 software (3D Medical Image Processing Software, Materialize, Leuven, Belgium).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform 3D reconstruction of anatomical structures from μCT image stacks using specialized software (MIMICS).
outputs: 3D models of anatomical structures (e.g., braincase endocast, inner ear)

steps.description: The visualization of 3D models was also conducted with AVIZO v. 8.0.0 software (Visualization Sciences Group, Burlington, MA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize 3D models using specialized visualization software (AVIZO).

steps.description: All the specimens μCT-scanned, or surface scanned will be available on MorphoMuseum for 3D models and on MorphoSource for μCT-scans (see dedicated part in the present volume).
steps.searchable_summary: Archive and make digital data (3D models, μCT scans) available on online repositories (MorphoMuseum, MorphoSource).
